output.final_deliverable:
Missing required evidence from page visits.

The merged results only include search results (URLs and snippets) but no visited-page content. The mandate requires visiting official Docker documentation pages and extracting: (1) what Docker is, (2) latest stable version number, (3) three key features/capabilities, and (4) an installation guide link—each supported by inline citations from visited URLs.

What I can list from the merged search results (not sufficient to comply):
- Candidate official “What is Docker?” page: https://docs.docker.com/get-started/docker-overview/
- Candidate installation guide: https://docs.docker.com/engine/install/
- Candidate release notes pages for version evidence: https://docs.docker.com/engine/release-notes/29/ and https://docs.docker.com/engine/release-notes/28/

To complete the task, the missing pieces are:
- Direct quotations/confirmed statements from visited docs.docker.com pages for the definition, features/capabilities, and the latest stable version number.
- Verified installation guide URL from a visited page (though a likely candidate is already identified above).

No inline URL citations can be provided because no pages were actually visited in the provided merged results.
output.action_summary: The provided merged results contain only search listings and no visited-page evidence. Because the mandate requires visiting official Docker documentation pages and citing them, the definition, latest stable version, key features/capabilities, and installation link cannot be extracted with compliant citations from the current evidence.

merged_results.title: Search for official Docker documentation pages that contain definition, features/capabilities, and installation guide links

result.query: site:docs.docker.com Docker what is Docker features capabilities installation guide
result.intent: Find official Docker documentation URLs (docs.docker.com) that define Docker, list key features/capabilities, and provide installation guide pages.

results.title: What is Docker? | Docker Docs
results.description: Docker provides the ability to package and run an application in a loosely isolated environment called a container. The isolation and security let you run many containers simultaneously on a given host.

results.title: Docker Desktop | Docker Docs
results.description: Install Docker Desktop on Mac, Windows, or Linux. Navigate Docker Desktop. Find information about Networking, Docker VMM, WSL, and more. Find out about new features, improvements, and bug fixes.

results.title: Linux | Docker Docs
results.description: It includes Docker Engine as this is the core technology that powers Docker containers. Docker Desktop for Linux also comes with additional features like Docker Scout and Docker Extensions.

results.title: Get started | Docker Docs
results.description: What is MCP Toolkit? How do I create an org? ... You've reached the maximum of questions per thread. For better answer quality, start a new thread. Start a new thread · Answers are generated based on the documentation. ... If you're new to Docker, this section guides you through the essential resources to get started.

results.title: Install | Docker Docs
results.description: Learn how to choose the best method for you to install Docker Engine. This client-server application is available on Linux, Mac, Windows, and as a static binary.

results.title: Docker Docs
results.description: How do I get started with Docker? Can I run my AI agent in a sandbox? What is a container? What are Docker Hardened Images? Why should I use Docker Compose? Learn Docker basics. Optimize your development workflows with Docker. Install, set up, configure, and use Docker products.

results.title: Docker Engine | Docker Docs
results.description: Learn how to install the open source Docker Engine for your distribution. Use persistent data with Docker containers. Manage network connections between containers. Learn how to view and read container logs. Tidy up unused resources. Delve into the configuration options of the Docker daemon. Run Docker without root privileges. Find out what features of Docker Engine you should stop using.

results.title: Introduction | Docker Docs
results.description: Embark on a comprehensive learning path to understand Docker and containerization, beginning with foundational concepts and installation procedures. Progress through hands-on exercises that cover essential Docker commands, image creation, and container orchestration. ... In this guide series, you will gain hands-on experience with Docker, starting with installing and setting up Docker Desktop on your local machine...

results.title: Ubuntu | Docker Docs
results.description: The DEB package includes a post-install script that completes additional setup steps automatically. ... Sets the capability on the Docker Desktop binary to map privileged ports and set resource limits. Adds a DNS name for Kubernetes to /etc/hosts.

results.title: Running containers | Docker Docs
results.description: Instead, the feature attempts to ensure that, when memory is heavily contended for, memory is allocated based on the reservation hints/setup.

merged_results.title: Search official Docker documentation for the latest stable Docker version number (release notes / engine or desktop version)

result.query: site:docs.docker.com latest stable version Docker version release notes
result.intent: Locate the official Docker docs page(s) that state the latest stable version number (e.g., Docker Engine or Docker Desktop) so we can extract an exact version.

results.title: Release notes | Docker Docs
results.description: Release notes for Docker Home, the Admin Console, billing, security, and subscription features

results.title: Engine v28 | Docker Docs
results.description: Update API version to 1.51. moby/moby#50145 · GET /images/json now sets the value of the Containers field for all images to the count of containers using the image. moby/moby#50146 · Empty/nil image config fields in the GET /images/{name}/json ...

results.title: Engine v24.0 | Docker Docs
results.description: Warn when pulling Docker Image Format v1, and Docker Image manifest version 2, schema 1 images from all registries. moby/moby#46290 · Fix live-restore of volumes with custom volume options. moby/moby#46366 · Fix incorrectly dropping capabilities bits when running a container as a non-root user (note: this change was already effectively present due to a regression).

results.title: Engine v29 | Docker Docs
results.description: Fix output format of the ApiVersion and MinApiVersion fields in docker version --format=json to align with previous versions. docker/cli#6648 · Fix a bug preventing DNS resolution of containers attached to non swarm-scoped networks once the node has joined a Swarm cluster. moby/moby#51515 ... For a full list of pull requests and changes in this release, refer to the relevant GitHub milestones: ... This release includes several breaking changes and deprecations. Review the release notes carefully before upgrading.

results.title: Engine v25.0 | Docker Docs
results.description: For a full list of pull requests ... MAC address is preserved. moby/moby#47304 ... Containers created with Docker Engine version 25.0.0 may have duplicate MAC addresses....

results.title: Docker Engine 23.0 release notes
results.description: For a full list of pull requests and changes in this release, refer to the relevant GitHub milestones: ... Fix containers not starting if the kernel has AppArmor enabled, but apparmor_parser is not available. moby/moby#44942 · Fix BuildKit-enabled builds with inline caching causing the daemon to crash. moby/moby#44944 · Fix BuildKit improperly loading cached layers created by previous versions.

results.title: Engine v26.1 | Docker Docs
results.description: Changes to the Engine API, see API version history. Fix an issue where the CLI process would sometimes hang when a container failed to start. docker/cli#5062 · Update Go runtime to 1.21.10. moby/moby#47806 ... For a full list of pull requests and changes in this release, refer to the relevant GitHub milestones:

results.title: Engine v27 | Docker Docs
results.description: ... Deprecated and removed features, see Deprecated Engine Features. Changes to the Engine API, see Engine API version history. Release notes for Docker Engine version 27.5 releases.

results.title: Engine v26.0 | Docker Docs
results.description: This page describes the latest changes, additions, known issues, and fixes for Docker Engine version 26.0. ... Deprecated and removed features, see Deprecated Engine Features. Changes to the Engine API, see Engine API version history.

results.title: Dockerfile release notes
results.description: This is a first version of BuildKit with signed release images and artifacts built using Docker Github Builder
